title: How structural patterns of species interactions affect response diversity
summary: Explores how ecological network structure and response diversity groups affect ecosystem resilience.
methods_or_focus: Community detection; ecological network simulations; generated and empirical network data; interactive visualisation.

title: Using soundscapes to reveal how species interaction networks shape response diversity
summary: Uses acoustic monitoring to infer species interactions and test how biotic and abiotic drivers shape response diversity.
methods_or_focus: AI pipelines for acoustic recordings; interaction networks; urbanisation gradients; typhoon disturbance data from OIST.

title: The effect and fate of response diversity under environmental change
summary: Tests how phytoplankton response diversity stabilises biomass production and changes under press and pulse warming.
methods_or_focus: Thermal reaction norms; metacommunity experiments; heatwaves; legacy effects in plankton communities.

title: How can we measure response diversity at scale across taxa?
summary: Develops field technologies and methods for monitoring response diversity across taxa in managed and unmanaged ecosystems.
methods_or_focus: Camera and acoustic sensor arrays; disturbance monitoring; restoration monitoring; comparison with biodiversity credit metrics.

title: Do biotic interactions homogenise responses and reduce response diversity?
summary: Tests how facilitation competition predation and parasitism shape organismal responses and response diversity in freshwater communities.
methods_or_focus: Microcosm and mesocosm experiments; multi-species assemblages; temperature treatments; direct and indirect interactions.

title: A general theory of response diversity effects on stability and coexistence
summary: Develops theory linking response diversity mean response temporal stability and coexistence across environmental drivers.
methods_or_focus: Community model structures; parameter databases; Lotka-Volterra and consumer-resource models; simulations across dynamical regimes.

title: Mean response and response diversity predict impact across environmental contexts
summary: Tests whether response diversity and mean response predict ecological impacts across drivers timescales and ecosystem types.
methods_or_focus: Single-species bioassays; algae and protist microcosms; pulse and press disturbances; statistical models.

title: Disruption of compensatory dynamics under extreme climatic events
summary: Assesses how extreme climatic events disrupt compensatory dynamics and vegetation stability across scales.
methods_or_focus: Long-term vegetation and trait data; phenology and abundance; climatic extremes; compensatory dynamics.

title: Boreal forest and tundra plant communities under environmental change
summary: Studies response diversity and stability in boreal forest and tundra plant communities along environmental gradients.
methods_or_focus: Functional traits; tundra and boreal field systems; remote sensing; environmental change gradients.

title: Restoration of co-invading alien plant species
summary: Investigates how response diversity among co-invading alien plants affects persistence restoration and management outcomes.
methods_or_focus: Greenhouse and field experiments; co-invasion dynamics; contaminated post-industrial landscapes; restoration applications.

title: Integrating response diversity into policy
summary: Analyses how response diversity concepts and indicators can be integrated into policy and monitoring frameworks.
methods_or_focus: Policy document analysis; biodiversity monitoring; nature restoration policy; policy briefs.

title: Opportunities and barriers for response-diversity-enabled policy
summary: Explores trade-offs barriers and opportunities for using response diversity in sustainability policy and decision-making.
methods_or_focus: Agent-based models; socio-economic trade-offs; corporate sustainability reporting; policy implementation barriers.

title: Physiological basis and consequences of response diversity
summary: Tests how physiological responses underpin response diversity and ecological stability in marine phytoplankton.
methods_or_focus: Phytoplankton experiments; thermal physiology; community assembly; response and effect traits; mechanistic models.
